prompt:
Quick review of this SQLAlchemy change for our user lookup?

```diff
+# app/repositories/user.py
+def find_by_email(session, email: str):
+    return (
+        session.query(User)
+        .filter(func.lower(User.email) == email.lower())
+        .one_or_none()
+    )
```

The `users` table has ~12M rows and there's already a unique B-tree index on `email`.

assertions.text: Identifies func.lower(User.email) as a function on the column that prevents using the existing email index (forces a sequential scan)
Explains the existing B-tree index is on the raw email value, so it cannot serve lower(email)
Recommends a concrete fix: a functional/expression index on lower(email), normalizing email on write, or citext
States the tradeoff of the recommended fix (e.g. functional index taxes writes, or normalization needs a migration)
